title: Uganda First-Time National Identification Registration (NIRA Form 3)
description: The National Identification and Registration Authority's (NIRA) "Form 3 — Application for Registration and National Identification Card" (First-Time Registration), under the Registration of Persons Act, 2015, used by an individual — a child or an adult — applying for a Ugandan National Identification Number (NIN) and National ID card for the first time. Closes Uganda's National ID & Civic Documents vertical (3 of 6), alongside the already-published `ug/mowt/driving-licence-application` (DMV) and `ug/ursb/business-name-registration-individual` (Business Formation). This schema models every applicant-supplied field across the form's own Part A (Personal Information; Place of Residence; Place of Birth; Place of Origin, Citizenship and Passport Information), Part B (Voters Information and Spouse Details, the source's own header marking Part B "to be filled for Adult registration only"), Part C (the applicant's own family particulars: Father's, Mother's, and Grandparent(s)/Introducer details, and Particulars of Applicant's Children), and Part E (Declaration). It excludes, in full: the Registration Location Code and Application ID at the top of Part A (per the form's own "FORM FILLING INFORMATION GUIDE" on page 4, these are filled by the "REGN OFFICER" or system-generated, never applicant-supplied); Part D in its entirety ("Form Section to be retained by the Registration Officer", a condensed duplicate of Parts A-C kept for the officer's own retained copy, not new applicant data); and Part F in its entirety ("FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY" — the registration officer's own citizenship assessment, documentary-proof ticks, and signature/date). Completing and submitting this form is an applicant's own action performed with NIRA; this schema does not file the application itself, and the live source is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Republic of Uganda or NIRA.

source.documentRef: "Form 3 — Application for Registration and National Identification Card (First Time Registration)", National Identification and Registration Authority, native (searchable-text) PDF, 4 pages (the 4th page is the form's own "FORM FILLING INFORMATION GUIDE", a field-by-field completion guide, not a 4th page of the form itself), served unauthenticated from nira.go.ug's own media library.

verification.notes:
GOV-4353 (child of GOV-4351, "GovSchema Standard Research"), closing the last item of Uganda's pre-scouted 3-of-6 backlog (CATALOG.md Known Gaps entry 0e) after `ug/mowt/driving-licence-application` (GOV-4335/4337) and `ug/ursb/business-name-registration-individual` (GOV-4343/4346) each independently reported `nira.go.ug` timing out on connect. Resolved this cycle: the bare `nira.go.ug` host still times out, but `www.nira.go.ug` resolves and serves the document over HTTP 200; the host also has an incomplete TLS certificate chain (a server misconfiguration, confirmed by `curl -k` succeeding where a strict-verification fetch fails), unrelated to the DNS/connect issue the bare host has.

Independently re-fetched and re-hashed rather than trusted from the issue brief alone: `https://www.nira.go.ug/media/2025/07/Form-3-NIRA-First-Time-Registration-Form.pdf`, HTTP 200, 312,831 bytes, sha256 `37e7df2b2800bd6e2e210944cd02f1c08ed265c924b2775fedc1b70cb54e4623` — byte-for-byte and hash-for-hash match against the issue's own reported figures. Confirmed mechanically: the retrieved bytes begin `%PDF-1.7` and contain zero `/AcroForm`, `/Widget`, and `/FT` occurrences — a flat, print-and-fill specimen, not an interactive AcroForm PDF. Also confirmed `https://www.nira.go.ug/` itself resolves (HTTP 200, page `<title>NIRA | National Identification & Registration Authority</title>`), supporting the `authority.url`.

Extraction method: text extracted via `pdfjs-dist`'s `getTextContent()` API (vendored at `/tmp/node_modules/pdfjs-dist`) across all 4 pages, walking each page's text-run items by exact x/y position (not rounded y-bucketing alone) to reconstruct multi-column rows precisely. Page 4, the form's own "FORM FILLING INFORMATION GUIDE", was used as the primary source for field descriptions and for the applicant-vs-"REGN OFFICER" scoping calls, per the issue's own instruction, not just the issue's own summary. Two areas of ambiguous layout were additionally cross-checked by rendering pages 1 and 2 to PNG via `pdfjs-dist` + `node-canvas` at 2.5x scale: the Part C.10 children table (see Finding 7) and the multi-column Country/District/County/Sub-County/Parish-Ward/Village address grids (used to confirm the 2-column vs 3-column row layouts described in Finding 8).

Models 165 `fields[]` across 12 steps, all applicant-supplied (per the page-4 guide's own "REGN OFFICER" attribution). Excludes, in full: the Registration Location Code and Application ID at the top of Part A (guide: location code is "REGN OFFICER"-filled; Application ID "To be generated by the Application"); Part D in its entirety ("Form Section to be retained by the Registration Officer", a condensed duplicate of Parts A-C for the officer's own retained copy); and Part F in its entirety ("FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY" — citizenship assessment, documentary-proof ticks, the registration officer's own name/signature/date).

## Disclosed source-fidelity findings

1. **No per-field required/optional markers exist anywhere in Parts A-C, except where the source itself prints an explicit textual condition** (e.g. "Fill if By registration, Naturalisation or Dual"). In that absence, this schema marks a field `required: true` only where it captures core identifying or administrative-boundary data indispensable to a national identity registration (name, sex, date of birth, and the Country/District/County/Sub-County/Parish-Ward/Village hierarchy for Place of Residence/Birth/Origin, plus `citizenshipType`), and `required: false` for auxiliary demographic, contact, or descriptive data (contacts, education, profession, occupation, religion, disabilities, other/previous/maiden names, street/plot-level address detail, passport/file numbers, and every Part C family-member field — see Finding 5). This is a disclosed judgment call, not a source-stated rule.

2. **`registrationType` ("FORM 3 CHILD ADULT (Tick Appropriate)") gates Part B in its entirety**, per the source's own Part B header, "(to be filled for Adult registration only)". Modelled as a required 2-value enum; every Part B field carries a matching `requiredWhen`/`visibleWhen` on `registrationType equals ADULT`.

3. **The five "Disabilities" checkboxes (Blind, Deaf, Physical, Mental, Dumb) are modelled as five independent optional booleans, not a single-select enum.** More than one can apply simultaneously (e.g. Deaf and Dumb, or Blind and Physical) and the source prints no mutual-exclusivity marker between them; v0.3's field-type vocabulary has no multi-select/array primitive (GSP-0009, not yet accepted), so this follows the independent-boolean-per-checkbox precedent this registry's own `my/jpj/driving-licence-application` (`physicalDisability`, etc.) established for a comparable non-exclusive checkbox row.

4. **The Spouse Details block (Part B.6) is modelled `requiredWhen`/`visibleWhen` an `all` of `registrationType equals ADULT` and `maritalStatus equals MARRIED`, though the source itself states no such condition explicitly** — Part B's own header conditions the whole Part on Adult registration, but nothing on the form ties Spouse Details specifically to a Married marital status; this is the disclosed judgment call the issue itself flagged, made because a spouse-details block is not logically answerable by a Single/Divorced/Widowed/Separated applicant. Within the block, `spouseSurname`/`spouseGivenNames`/`spouseCitizenshipType`/`placeOfMarriage`/`dateOfMarriage`/`typeOfMarriage`/`marriageCertificateNumber`/`otherSpouses` inherit this same conditional requiredness; `spouseOtherNames`/`spousePreviousNames`/`spouseMaidenName`/`spouseNationalIdNumber`/`spouseDualCitizenship`/`spouseCitizenshipUnderArticle9` remain unconditionally optional even within the block (per Finding 1's general policy).

5. **The Father's, Mother's, and Grandparent(s)/Introducer blocks (Part C.7-9) carry no outer `requiredWhen`/`visibleWhen` gate at all — every field in all three blocks is unconditionally optional.** Section 9's own printed condition — "if information of both parents is not available for Applicants under Citizenship by Birth/ descent" — establishes that father/mother information is not always available, which is why the Grandparent/Introducer fallback exists; by the same logic this schema does not fabricate a `required: true` on any father/mother field either. GSP-0013's Condition grammar also has no "field is absent" leaf operator, so a conditional link from `grandparentSurname` to "father and mother details are both blank" could not be expressed even if the source stated it more mechanically. This mirrors the treatment the issue itself asked for.

6. **The `citizenshipCertificateNumber`/`otherNationality` conditional-requiredness wording is taken verbatim from the page-4 guide, not inferred**: "Certificate Number Fill if By registration, Naturalisation or Dual" and "Other Nationality If DUAL Citizen (Double Citizen) then fill Other Country". Applied identically to the applicant's own, Father's, and Mother's citizenship blocks, per the guide's own "CITIZENSHIP OF FATHER Citizenship Type Same as Citizenship of Applicant" (and the equivalent line for Mother). The Spouse Details block (Part B.6), however, prints no "Citizenship Certificate Number" line at all between its own article-9 tick and its "If Dual..." line — confirmed by re-extracting the exact y-ordered text run on page 1 (the applicant's, Father's, and Mother's blocks each print that line; Spouse's does not) — so `spouseCitizenshipCertificateNumber` is not modelled at all, an asymmetry disclosed here rather than silently "fixed" by adding a field the source does not print.

7. **Part C.10 "Particulars of Applicant's Children" is modelled as 4 bounded slots (`child1`..`child4`), not an unbounded array, because the source itself prints exactly 4 blank data rows beneath the table's own column headers** — confirmed by rendering page 2 to a PNG at 2.5x scale and measuring the printed row-divider line pixel positions (6 horizontal rules bounding 1 header row + 4 data rows, each ~15pt tall, ending well above the page-4 footer). This is a genuine literal-printed-row-count anchor, the convention this registry's prior bounded-repeating-slot precedents required, rather than the looser 1-2-slot "replicate this section" scoping this registry used for `zm/pacra/company-incorporation`'s unbounded director rows; v0.3 has no array/repeating-element primitive (GSP-0009, not yet accepted) so a fifth child exceeds this form's own printed capacity, a limitation disclosed here rather than silently dropped.

8. **Father's and Mother's Place of Residence "Street"/"House No." fields are modelled matching the applicant's own Place of Residence structure (`residenceStreet` + `residencePlotHouseNumber`), per the page-4 guide's own "Guidance same as Place of Residence" instruction for both parents, even though the raw extracted label text for this specific row differs slightly between the three blocks** (applicant: "Street" / "Plot / House No."; Father's printed row: "Street" / "Street/House No."; Mother's printed row folds Village/Street/House No. into one wider 3-column row rather than 2). Treated as the same two fields in all three blocks per the guide's own explicit equivalence, not fabricated from an assumption of visual similarity alone.

## Conformance

5 valid mock scenarios (a first-time child registrant with only Part A/C fields populated; a first-time adult single citizen-by-birth applicant with no children; an adult married-by-registration applicant with a spouse, one child, and a dual-citizenship declaration; an adult applicant relying on the Grandparent/Introducer fallback with both parents' blocks left blank; an adult applicant with all 4 children slots populated) plus mutation-control fixtures — one missing-required-field control for each statically-required field (`registrationType`, `surname`, `givenName`, `sex`, `dateOfBirth`, `residenceType`, `residenceCountry`, `residenceDistrict`, `residenceCounty`, `residenceSubCounty`, `residenceParishWard`, `residenceVillage`, `yearsLivedAtAddress`, `birthCountry`, `birthDistrict`, `birthCounty`, `birthSubCounty`, `birthParishWard`, `birthVillage`, `originCountry`, `originDistrict`, `originCounty`, `originSubCounty`, `originParishWard`, `originVillage`, `citizenshipType`, `declarantName`, `signature`, `date`), one missing-conditionally-required control for a representative subset of `requiredWhen` fields (`citizenshipCertificateNumber`, `otherNationality`, `preferredPollingStation`, `maritalStatus`, `spouseSurname`, `spouseCitizenshipType`, `spouseOtherNationality`, `placeOfMarriage`, `otherSpousesCount`, `fatherCitizenshipCertificateNumber`, `fatherOtherNationality`, `motherCitizenshipCertificateNumber`, `motherOtherNationality`), an invalid-enum control (`sex`), and an unknown-top-level-field control, are committed under `conformance/ug/nira/first-time-registration-application/1.0.0/`. An ephemeral, from-scratch conformance checker (deriving required and `requiredWhen`/`visibleWhen` rules directly from this schema's own `fields[]`, discarded after use, not committed) ran all fixtures: all valid scenarios at 0 errors, all mutation controls each raising exactly 1 error. Validated clean with `node tools/validate.mjs` and `node tools/validate-ajv.mjs`, individually and as part of the full registry run. `registry-index.json` regenerated via `npm run build-index` in `tools/govschema-client/`.

steps.title: Part A.4 — Place of Origin, Citizenship, and Passport Information
Part B.5 — Voters Information (Adult registration only)
Part B.6 — Spouse Details (Adult registration, married applicants only)
Part C.9 — Details of Grand Parent(s)/Introducer
Part C.10 — Particulars of Applicant's Children

fields.name: registrationType
fields.label: FORM 3 CHILD ADULT (Tick Appropriate)
fields.description: Whether this is a first-time registration for a child or an adult applicant. Gates Part B (Voter Information and Spouse Details), which the source form itself marks 'to be filled for Adult registration only'.
fields.sourceRef: "FORM 3 CHILD ADULT (Tick Appropriate)" — form header

fields.name: otherNames
fields.label: Other Names
fields.description: Middle name(s) or extra names, per the form-filling guide.
fields.sourceRef: PART A.1 Personal Information — "Other Names"

fields.name: maidenName
fields.label: Maiden Name
fields.description: Names carried before marriage, per the form-filling guide (guide: "Names carried before Marriage (Females Only)"). Not gated to sex or marital status: it applies only where the applicant has actually married and adopted a different name, a narrower condition than sex alone, so this is modelled as unconditionally optional rather than a requiredWhen on sex — a disclosed judgment call.
fields.sourceRef: PART A.1 Personal Information — "Maiden Name"

fields.name: grandparentSurname
fields.label: Surname
fields.description: Section 9 is itself printed conditionally: "if information of both parents is not available for Applicants under Citizenship by Birth/ descent". This schema models the whole block as unconditionally optional rather than encoding that condition against fatherSurname/motherSurname absence, since GSP-0013's Condition grammar has no "field is absent" leaf operator — a disclosed judgment call. See this schema's own verification.notes.

fields.name: child1Name
fields.label: Names of Child
fields.description: The source prints this table with exactly 4 blank data rows beneath its column headers (confirmed by rendering page 2 to an image and measuring the printed row-divider lines); this schema models 4 bounded child1..child4 slots matching that literal printed row count rather than an unbounded array, since v0.3's field model has no array/repeating-element primitive (GSP-0009, not yet accepted). A fifth or later child exceeds this form's own printed capacity.
